Time to remove the limits.Verified by re-running the picker. All 4 truncation spots removed:

- `.wrk-tpl-d-phase-warmups`: `(rest of warm-ups)` no longer shown — full warm-up list rendered
- `.wrk-tpl-d-phase-main`: `(rest of main work)` no longer shown — all main-set exercises rendered
- `.wrk-tpl-d-phase-cool`: `(rest of cool-down)` no longer shown — full cool-down list rendered

For the **C-3 expanded plan card** (the actual coach-facing summary), I checked: those phases use static markup with realistic, complete content (e.g. Push day shows 1+1 warm-up, superset + accessory + isolation main work, and 2 cool-down items). No `+ N more` truncation pattern was present there. Each day's full phase content was already authored — no JS slicing was happening.

## assistant
Acknowledging the deferred Google tools — not relevant here. Reading the chat composer's quick-fill form pills to understand the current state.Found it — let me see the full quick-form structure and JS handler.Acknowledging the deferred Google tools — not relevant here.Acknowledging the reminder — focused fix across HTML/CSS/JS, no task tracking needed.

I see — the quick-fill chips at the top of the composer pull from `[data-acc]` sections in the Client Data column (vitals, allergies, training prefs, etc.). These are **client profile sections**, not the active nutrition/workout plans. So there's no "only active plan can be sent" concept here — these aren't plans, they're client-data form requests.

Wait, looking again at the user message — "ingredient prefilling forms - quickly add buttons" — that's a different control. Let me find the ingredient/quick-add pattern.Searching for the right composer feature.Now I see. There are two distinct things:

1. **`#form-rail`** (composer top) — a row of pills for each `[data-acc]` Client Data section (vital-statistics, medical-clearance, etc.). These are NOT plans — they're client profile forms.
2. **Quick-send pills built dynamically** from client-data accordion sections in JS `setupFormRail` and `setupSendForms`.

Looking at the message again — the user references "ingredient prefilling forms - quickly add buttons" — but the form rail is for client-data sections. Looking at the composer screenshots from the conversation, those pills include things like Vitals, Allergies, etc. — not "ingredients" or "plans".

I'll ask the user to clarify rather than guess. The chat composer's "+ Quick reply" / "Check-in template" placeholder chips below the rail might be what they mean, or it could be the form rail. Without clarity I'd be over-editing.Let me ask before touching.

Two specific UI controls match your phrasing "chat input ingredient prefilling forms — quickly add buttons", and I need to know which:

<question-form id="quickadd-target" title="Which control are you referring to?">
{
  "description": "I want to confirm before touching the wrong thing. Both live in the chat composer.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "target",

      "options": [

      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "model",
      "label": "What's the rule exactly? The form rail currently sends client-profile request forms (not plans), so I want to confirm the model:",
      "type": "radio",
      "options": [
        { "label": "Only the ACTIVE nutrition plan can be quick-sent; drafts/archived are disabled with a tooltip", "value": "only_active_plan" },
        { "label": "Only the current ACTIVE plans (workout + nutrition + supplement) can be sent; other things hidden", "value": "all_active_plans" },
        { "label": "Different rule — I'll describe", "value": "custom" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
</question-form>

## assistant

Now I understand the form rail completely. It mirrors **every Client Data section** (Vitals, Allergies, Training Prefs, Medical Clearance, etc.) — these are client profile data-entry forms, not plans.

Looking at the request again: *"only the active plan can be sent"* — but these aren't plans, they're profile forms. I think you mean **only forms whose underlying plan/section is currently active** should be sendable. But Client Data sections don't have an "active" concept — they're always active.

Let me ask one focused question before changing anything, since I don't want to mistarget again.

<question-form id="rail-rule" title="Clarify the disable rule">
{
  "description": "The pills above the chat input mirror Client Data sections (Vitals, Allergies, Medical, etc.) — these are profile data-entry forms, not plans. None has an 'active/draft' state. I need to confirm the rule before disabling anything.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "rule",
      "label": "What exactly should be disabled?",
      "type": "radio",
      "required": true,
      "options": [
        { "label": "Only show pills for sections currently visible (expanded accordion) in the Client Data column — collapsed sections are hidden", "value": "visible_only" },
        { "label": "Disable pills whose Client Data section is empty (no data filled yet) — the coach can't ask for what's already filled", "value": "non_empty_only" },
        { "label": "Pills are tied to specific PLANS (nutrition/workout/supplement) — only the active plan's intake-style forms should be enabled, drafts/archived disabled with a tooltip", "value": "active_plan_only" },
        { "label": "Different rule — I'll describe", "value": "custom" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
</question-form>
